commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR suppresses denied exec approval follow-up agent turns, drops remote `exec.denied` node events before enqueue/heartbeat, and adds Chinese stop phrases to abort detection tests and primitives.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main still routes ordinary denied exec approval results into agent follow-up work, wakes sessions on node `exec.denied`, and lacks the reported `停下来` / `暂停` abort triggers.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the protocol docs and prevent unused denied-followup handoff registrations without changing the chosen denial-loop behavior.

target.title: fix(agents): stop denied exec followups
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: denied exec approvals and remote `exec.denied` node events could still feed agent-visible follow-up work, so a user denial could look like a new system prompt and keep the loop alive.
- Solution: make denied exec approvals terminal for ordinary sessions, and drop `exec.denied` node events before enqueue/heartbeat paths.
- What changed: direct delivery targets still get a concise denial notice, successful exec/request paths stay unchanged, and Chinese stop phrases now hit abort cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): command approval policy, allowlist behavior, successful exec notifications, and the external delivery contract for real chat channels.

## Motivation

- Users saying no to an exec request, or saying stop in Chinese, must stop the current execution path. A denial should not become model-visible work that can continue or re-request the same command.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69386
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: denied exec approvals no longer spawn agent follow-up turns, and `exec.denied` node events no longer enqueue or wake agent work.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway started in-process, real WebSocket operator client, real WebSocket node-role client, real device pairing, real node pairing, and real `node.invoke` `system.run` flow.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline script that starts the Gateway, connects both WebSocket clients to `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>/gateway`, pairs a device and node, invokes `node.invoke` for `system.run`, sends a real `node.event` `exec.denied`, and reads `systemEvents.peekSystemEvents(sessionKey)`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "real gateway + real websocket operator/node clients",
  "port": 49370,
  "nodeId": "287821324ba8d51176a38bb12a0871728ba34aea1630e3a4022361f444765e31",
  "operatorDeniedResult": "UNAVAILABLE: SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required",
  "deniedRpcPayload": { "ok": true },
  "deniedEvents": []
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the Gateway accepted the real denied exec event and left the session system-event queue empty, so no agent wakeup or follow-up work was created.
- What was not tested: live third-party chat delivery; this proof covers the Gateway/WebSocket/node runtime path, and focused tests cover direct denial copy and stop phrase parsing.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #69386 contains the user-visible loop where denial/stop did not terminate the flow.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: denied exec outcomes were treated like follow-up-worthy delivery failures or node exec lifecycle events, so denial information could be converted into new agent-visible work.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage did not assert that a denied exec approval is terminal for ordinary sessions or that `exec.denied` skips enqueue/heartbeat.
- Contributing context (if known): stop phrase matching did not include common Chinese stop commands used in the report.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/abort.test.ts`, `src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: denied exec approval is terminal without external delivery, direct delivery gets one concise denial message, `exec.denied` does not enqueue or heartbeat, and Chinese stop phrases are recognized.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: each test targets the boundary where denied exec output previously escaped into follow-up work.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none covered the denied-as-terminal case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Denied exec approvals now stop ordinary agent follow-up instead of generating more work. Chinese stop phrases such as `停下来` and `暂停` now trigger abort cleanup.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[exec denied] -> [follow-up/system event] -> [agent can continue]

After:
[exec denied] -> [terminal denial result] -> [no agent wakeup]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the command execution surface is reduced for denied paths. The mitigation is scoped to denied outcomes only; approval, allowlist, successful exec, and external direct delivery behavior remain covered.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Darwin 25.4.0 arm64
- Runtime/container: local Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): local Gateway WebSocket operator + node-role clients
- Relevant config (redacted): temporary local Gateway config generated by the proof script; no secrets

### Steps

1. Start Gateway in-process on an ephemeral local port.
2. Connect real operator and node WebSocket clients.
3. Pair the device and node.
4. Invoke `system.run` through `node.invoke` and deny the approval through a real `node.event` `exec.denied`.
5. Read the session system-event queue.

### Expected

- Denied exec result is reported to the operator, and no system event or heartbeat is created for the denied event.

### Actual

- `operatorDeniedResult` was `UNAVAILABLE: SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required`.
- `deniedRpcPayload` was `{ "ok": true }`.
- `deniedEvents` was `[]`.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real Gateway/WebSocket/node denied exec path, focused regression tests, build, formatting, and committed autoreview.
- Edge cases checked: direct denial delivery target still gets a concise message; successful exec result follow-up remains; Chinese stop commands trigger abort cleanup.
- What you did **not** verify: live third-party chat channel delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a real external chat denial could be swallowed if treated as an ordinary non-delivery session.
  - Mitigation: external best-effort delivery targets still receive a direct denial message; only non-deliverable ordinary sessions suppress follow-up work.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/abort-primitives.ts src/auto-reply/reply/abort.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/abort.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts -t "does not spawn a gateway followup agent when approval is denied"`
- `pnpm build`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`
- Real behavior proof described above with a real Gateway and real WebSocket clients.
